subject.summary: Apply or dry-run high-level authoring intents through the shared authoring core.

constraints.summary: Authoring placement.anchor is required when placement.mode is before or after.
Authoring placement.anchor must be omitted when placement.mode is first or last.
All scaffold and scaffold-edge local_id values must be unique within one authoring request.
Authoring local_id references must resolve to nodes created earlier in the same request.
Handle-based authoring references are valid only against the supplied base_revision.
Committed created_targets from author results are safe continuation surfaces only for the returned resulting_revision.
Dry-run created_targets from author results are informational only and must not be reused in later requests.

continuation.summary: When author returns a resulting_revision, that revision is the next valid base_revision for follow-on work.
Committed created_targets from author are safe continuation surfaces only for the returned resulting_revision.
Dry-run created_targets from author are informational only and must not be reused in later requests.
